  window.builderIoAbTest = function updateCookiesAndStyles(contentId, variants, isHydrationTarget, isAngularSDK) {
  function getAndSetVariantId() {
    function setCookie(name, value, days) {
      let expires = '';
      if (days) {
        const date = new Date();
        date.setTime(date.getTime() + days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
        expires = '; expires=' + date.toUTCString();
      }
      document.cookie = name + '=' + (value || '') + expires + '; path=/' + '; Secure; SameSite=None';
    }
    function getCookie(name) {
      const nameEQ = name + '=';
      const ca = document.cookie.split(';');
      for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
        let c = ca[i];
        while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') c = c.substring(1, c.length);
        if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
      }
      return null;
    }
    const cookieName = `builder.tests.${contentId}`;
    const variantInCookie = getCookie(cookieName);
    const availableIDs = variants.map(vr => vr.id).concat(contentId);
    if (variantInCookie && availableIDs.includes(variantInCookie)) {
      return variantInCookie;
    }
    let n = 0;
    const random = Math.random();
    for (let i = 0; i < variants.length; i++) {
      const variant = variants[i];
      const testRatio = variant.testRatio;
      n += testRatio;
      if (random < n) {
        setCookie(cookieName, variant.id);
        return variant.id;
      }
    }
    setCookie(cookieName, contentId);
    return contentId;
  }
  const winningVariantId = getAndSetVariantId();
  let styleEl = document.currentScript?.previousElementSibling;
  if (isAngularSDK) {
    styleEl = document.currentScript?.parentElement?.previousElementSibling?.querySelector('style');
  }
  if (isHydrationTarget) {
    styleEl.remove();
    const thisScriptEl = document.currentScript;
    thisScriptEl?.remove();
  } else {
    const newStyleStr = variants.concat({
      id: contentId
    }).filter(variant => variant.id !== winningVariantId).map(value => {
      return `.variant-${value.id} {  display: none; }
        `;
    }).join('');
    styleEl.innerHTML = newStyleStr;
  }
}
  window.builderIoRenderContent = function updateVariantVisibility(variantContentId, defaultContentId, isHydrationTarget) {
  if (!navigator.cookieEnabled) {
    return;
  }
  function getCookie(name) {
    const nameEQ = name + '=';
    const ca = document.cookie.split(';');
    for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
      let c = ca[i];
      while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') c = c.substring(1, c.length);
      if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
    }
    return null;
  }
  const cookieName = `builder.tests.${defaultContentId}`;
  const winningVariant = getCookie(cookieName);
  const parentDiv = document.currentScript?.parentElement;
  const isDefaultContent = variantContentId === defaultContentId;
  const isWinningVariant = winningVariant === variantContentId;
  if (isWinningVariant && !isDefaultContent) {
    parentDiv?.removeAttribute('hidden');
    parentDiv?.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  } else if (!isWinningVariant && isDefaultContent) {
    parentDiv?.setAttribute('hidden', 'true');
    parentDiv?.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  }
  if (isHydrationTarget) {
    if (!isWinningVariant) {
      parentDiv?.remove();
    }
    const thisScriptEl = document.currentScript;
    thisScriptEl?.remove();
  }
  return;
}
  </script><script data-id="builderio-init-personalization-variants-fns" nonce="">
  window.filterWithCustomTargeting = function filterWithCustomTargeting(userAttributes, query, startDate, endDate) {
  function isString(val) {
    return typeof val === 'string';
  }
  function isNumber(val) {
    return typeof val === 'number';
  }
  function objectMatchesQuery(userattr, query) {
    const result = (() => {
      const property = query.property;
      const operator = query.operator;
      let testValue = query.value;
      if (query && query.property === 'urlPath' && query.value && typeof query.value === 'string' && query.value !== '/' && query.value.endsWith('/')) {
        testValue = query.value.slice(0, -1);
      }
      if (!(property && operator)) {
        return true;
      }
      if (Array.isArray(testValue)) {
        if (operator === 'isNot') {
          return testValue.every(val => objectMatchesQuery(userattr, {
            property,
            operator,
            value: val
          }));
        }
        return !!testValue.find(val => objectMatchesQuery(userattr, {
          property,
          operator,
          value: val
        }));
      }
      const value = userattr[property];
      if (Array.isArray(value)) {
        return value.includes(testValue);
      }
      switch (operator) {
        case 'is':
          return value === testValue;
        case 'isNot':
          return value !== testValue;
        case 'contains':
          return (isString(value) || Array.isArray(value)) && value.includes(String(testValue));
        case 'startsWith':
          return isString(value) && value.startsWith(String(testValue));
        case 'endsWith':
          return isString(value) && value.endsWith(String(testValue));
        case 'greaterThan':
          return isNumber(value) && isNumber(testValue) && value > testValue;
        case 'lessThan':
          return isNumber(value) && isNumber(testValue) && value < testValue;
        case 'greaterThanOrEqualTo':
          return isNumber(value) && isNumber(testValue) && value >= testValue;
        case 'lessThanOrEqualTo':
          return isNumber(value) && isNumber(testValue) && value <= testValue;
        default:
          return false;
      }
    })();
    return result;
  }
  const item = {
    query,
    startDate,
    endDate
  };
  const now = userAttributes.date && new Date(userAttributes.date) || new Date();
  if (item.startDate && new Date(item.startDate) > now) {
    return false;
  } else if (item.endDate && new Date(item.endDate) < now) {
    return false;
  }
  if (!item.query || !item.query.length) {
    return true;
  }
  return item.query.every(filter => {
    return objectMatchesQuery(userAttributes, filter);
  });
}
  window.builderIoPersonalization = function getPersonalizedVariant(variants, blockId, isHydrationTarget, locale) {
  if (!navigator.cookieEnabled) {
    return;
  }
  function getCookie(name) {
    const nameEQ = name + '=';
    const ca = document.cookie.split(';');
    for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
      let c = ca[i];
      while (c.charAt(0) == ' ') c = c.substring(1, c.length);
      if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) == 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
    }
    return null;
  }
  const attributes = JSON.parse(getCookie('builder.userAttributes') || '{}');
  if (locale) {
    attributes.locale = locale;
  }
  const winningVariantIndex = variants?.findIndex(function (variant) {
    return window.filterWithCustomTargeting(attributes, variant.query, variant.startDate, variant.endDate);
  });
  const parentDiv = document.currentScript?.parentElement;
  const variantId = parentDiv?.getAttribute('data-variant-id');
  const isDefaultVariant = variantId === `${blockId}-default`;
  const isWinningVariant = winningVariantIndex !== -1 && variantId === `${blockId}-${winningVariantIndex}` || winningVariantIndex === -1 && isDefaultVariant;
  if (isWinningVariant && !isDefaultVariant) {
    parentDiv?.removeAttribute('hidden');
    parentDiv?.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  } else if (!isWinningVariant && isDefaultVariant) {
    parentDiv?.setAttribute('hidden', 'true');
    parentDiv?.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  }
  if (isHydrationTarget) {
    if (!isWinningVariant) {
      const itsStyleEl = parentDiv?.previousElementSibling;
      if (itsStyleEl) {
        itsStyleEl.remove();
      }
      parentDiv?.remove();
    }
    const thisScript = document.currentScript;
    if (thisScript) {
      thisScript.remove();
    }
  }
}
  window.updateVisibilityStylesScript = function updateVisibilityStylesScript(variants, blockId, isHydrationTarget, locale) {
  function getCookie(name) {
    const nameEQ = name + '=';
    const ca = document.cookie.split(';');
    for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
      let c = ca[i];
      while (c.charAt(0) == ' ') c = c.substring(1, c.length);
      if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) == 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
    }
    return null;
  }
  const visibilityStylesEl = document.currentScript?.previousElementSibling;
  if (!visibilityStylesEl) {
    return;
  }
  if (isHydrationTarget) {
    visibilityStylesEl.remove();
    const currentScript = document.currentScript;
    if (currentScript) {
      currentScript.remove();
    }
  } else {
    const attributes = JSON.parse(getCookie('builder.userAttributes') || '{}');
    if (locale) {
      attributes.locale = locale;
    }
    const winningVariantIndex = variants?.findIndex(function (variant) {
      return window.filterWithCustomTargeting(attributes, variant.query, variant.startDate, variant.endDate);
    });
    if (winningVariantIndex !== -1) {
      let newStyleStr = variants?.map((_, index) => {
        if (index === winningVariantIndex) return '';
        return `div[data-variant-id="${blockId}-${index}"] { display: none !important; } `;
      }).join('') || '';
      newStyleStr += `div[data-variant-id="${blockId}-default"] { display: none !important; } `;
      visibilityStylesEl.innerHTML = newStyleStr;
    }
  }
}

  window.builderIoAbTest = function updateCookiesAndStyles(contentId, variants, isHydrationTarget, isAngularSDK) {
  function getAndSetVariantId() {
    function setCookie(name, value, days) {
      let expires = '';
      if (days) {
        const date = new Date();
        date.setTime(date.getTime() + days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
        expires = '; expires=' + date.toUTCString();
      }
      document.cookie = name + '=' + (value || '') + expires + '; path=/' + '; Secure; SameSite=None';
    }
    function getCookie(name) {
      const nameEQ = name + '=';
      const ca = document.cookie.split(';');
      for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
        let c = ca[i];
        while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') c = c.substring(1, c.length);
        if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
      }
      return null;
    }
    const cookieName = `builder.tests.${contentId}`;
    const variantInCookie = getCookie(cookieName);
    const availableIDs = variants.map(vr => vr.id).concat(contentId);
    if (variantInCookie && availableIDs.includes(variantInCookie)) {
      return variantInCookie;
    }
    let n = 0;
    const random = Math.random();
    for (let i = 0; i < variants.length; i++) {
      const variant = variants[i];
      const testRatio = variant.testRatio;
      n += testRatio;
      if (random < n) {
        setCookie(cookieName, variant.id);
        return variant.id;
      }
    }
    setCookie(cookieName, contentId);
    return contentId;
  }
  const winningVariantId = getAndSetVariantId();
  let styleEl = document.currentScript?.previousElementSibling;
  if (isAngularSDK) {
    styleEl = document.currentScript?.parentElement?.previousElementSibling?.querySelector('style');
  }
  if (isHydrationTarget) {
    styleEl.remove();
    const thisScriptEl = document.currentScript;
    thisScriptEl?.remove();
  } else {
    const newStyleStr = variants.concat({
      id: contentId
    }).filter(variant => variant.id !== winningVariantId).map(value => {
      return `.variant-${value.id} {  display: none; }
        `;
    }).join('');
    styleEl.innerHTML = newStyleStr;
  }
}
  window.builderIoRenderContent = function updateVariantVisibility(variantContentId, defaultContentId, isHydrationTarget) {
  if (!navigator.cookieEnabled) {
    return;
  }
  function getCookie(name) {
    const nameEQ = name + '=';
    const ca = document.cookie.split(';');
    for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
      let c = ca[i];
      while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') c = c.substring(1, c.length);
      if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
    }
    return null;
  }
  const cookieName = `builder.tests.${defaultContentId}`;
  const winningVariant = getCookie(cookieName);
  const parentDiv = document.currentScript?.parentElement;
  const isDefaultContent = variantContentId === defaultContentId;
  const isWinningVariant = winningVariant === variantContentId;
  if (isWinningVariant && !isDefaultContent) {
    parentDiv?.removeAttribute('hidden');
    parentDiv?.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  } else if (!isWinningVariant && isDefaultContent) {
    parentDiv?.setAttribute('hidden', 'true');
    parentDiv?.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  }
  if (isHydrationTarget) {
    if (!isWinningVariant) {
      parentDiv?.remove();
    }
    const thisScriptEl = document.currentScript;
    thisScriptEl?.remove();
  }
  return;
}
  </script><script data-id="builderio-init-personalization-variants-fns" nonce="">
  window.filterWithCustomTargeting = function filterWithCustomTargeting(userAttributes, query, startDate, endDate) {
  function isString(val) {
    return typeof val === 'string';
  }
  function isNumber(val) {
    return typeof val === 'number';
  }
  function objectMatchesQuery(userattr, query) {
    const result = (() => {
      const property = query.property;
      const operator = query.operator;
      let testValue = query.value;
      if (query && query.property === 'urlPath' && query.value && typeof query.value === 'string' && query.value !== '/' && query.value.endsWith('/')) {
        testValue = query.value.slice(0, -1);
      }
      if (!(property && operator)) {
        return true;
      }
      if (Array.isArray(testValue)) {
        if (operator === 'isNot') {
          return testValue.every(val => objectMatchesQuery(userattr, {
            property,
            operator,
            value: val
          }));
        }
        return !!testValue.find(val => objectMatchesQuery(userattr, {
          property,
          operator,
          value: val
        }));
      }
      const value = userattr[property];
      if (Array.isArray(value)) {
        return value.includes(testValue);
      }
      switch (operator) {
        case 'is':
          return value === testValue;
        case 'isNot':
          return value !== testValue;
        case 'contains':
          return (isString(value) || Array.isArray(value)) && value.includes(String(testValue));
        case 'startsWith':
          return isString(value) && value.startsWith(String(testValue));
        case 'endsWith':
          return isString(value) && value.endsWith(String(testValue));
        case 'greaterThan':
          return isNumber(value) && isNumber(testValue) && value > testValue;
        case 'lessThan':
          return isNumber(value) && isNumber(testValue) && value < testValue;
        case 'greaterThanOrEqualTo':
          return isNumber(value) && isNumber(testValue) && value >= testValue;
        case 'lessThanOrEqualTo':
          return isNumber(value) && isNumber(testValue) && value <= testValue;
        default:
          return false;
      }
    })();
    return result;
  }
  const item = {
    query,
    startDate,
    endDate
  };
  const now = userAttributes.date && new Date(userAttributes.date) || new Date();
  if (item.startDate && new Date(item.startDate) > now) {
    return false;
  } else if (item.endDate && new Date(item.endDate) < now) {
    return false;
  }
  if (!item.query || !item.query.length) {
    return true;
  }
  return item.query.every(filter => {
    return objectMatchesQuery(userAttributes, filter);
  });
}
  window.builderIoPersonalization = function getPersonalizedVariant(variants, blockId, isHydrationTarget, locale) {
  if (!navigator.cookieEnabled) {
    return;
  }
  function getCookie(name) {
    const nameEQ = name + '=';
    const ca = document.cookie.split(';');
    for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
      let c = ca[i];
      while (c.charAt(0) == ' ') c = c.substring(1, c.length);
      if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) == 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
    }
    return null;
  }
  const attributes = JSON.parse(getCookie('builder.userAttributes') || '{}');
  if (locale) {
    attributes.locale = locale;
  }
  const winningVariantIndex = variants?.findIndex(function (variant) {
    return window.filterWithCustomTargeting(attributes, variant.query, variant.startDate, variant.endDate);
  });
  const parentDiv = document.currentScript?.parentElement;
  const variantId = parentDiv?.getAttribute('data-variant-id');
  const isDefaultVariant = variantId === `${blockId}-default`;
  const isWinningVariant = winningVariantIndex !== -1 && variantId === `${blockId}-${winningVariantIndex}` || winningVariantIndex === -1 && isDefaultVariant;
  if (isWinningVariant && !isDefaultVariant) {
    parentDiv?.removeAttribute('hidden');
    parentDiv?.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  } else if (!isWinningVariant && isDefaultVariant) {
    parentDiv?.setAttribute('hidden', 'true');
    parentDiv?.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  }
  if (isHydrationTarget) {
    if (!isWinningVariant) {
      const itsStyleEl = parentDiv?.previousElementSibling;
      if (itsStyleEl) {
        itsStyleEl.remove();
      }
      parentDiv?.remove();
    }
    const thisScript = document.currentScript;
    if (thisScript) {
      thisScript.remove();
    }
  }
}
  window.updateVisibilityStylesScript = function updateVisibilityStylesScript(variants, blockId, isHydrationTarget, locale) {
  function getCookie(name) {
    const nameEQ = name + '=';
    const ca = document.cookie.split(';');
    for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
      let c = ca[i];
      while (c.charAt(0) == ' ') c = c.substring(1, c.length);
      if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) == 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
    }
    return null;
  }
  const visibilityStylesEl = document.currentScript?.previousElementSibling;
  if (!visibilityStylesEl) {
    return;
  }
  if (isHydrationTarget) {
    visibilityStylesEl.remove();
    const currentScript = document.currentScript;
    if (currentScript) {
      currentScript.remove();
    }
  } else {
    const attributes = JSON.parse(getCookie('builder.userAttributes') || '{}');
    if (locale) {
      attributes.locale = locale;
    }
    const winningVariantIndex = variants?.findIndex(function (variant) {
      return window.filterWithCustomTargeting(attributes, variant.query, variant.startDate, variant.endDate);
    });
    if (winningVariantIndex !== -1) {
      let newStyleStr = variants?.map((_, index) => {
        if (index === winningVariantIndex) return '';
        return `div[data-variant-id="${blockId}-${index}"] { display: none !important; } `;
      }).join('') || '';
      newStyleStr += `div[data-variant-id="${blockId}-default"] { display: none !important; } `;
      visibilityStylesEl.innerHTML = newStyleStr;
    }
  }
}
  </script><div builder-content-id="cc72abb7f46248bdb3ef23ab484b58ff" builder-model="page" class="variant-cc72abb7f46248bdb3ef23ab484b58ff"><style data-id="builderio-content" nonce="">.builder-button {
  all: unset;
}

  margin: 0;
}

    font-size: var(--text-body-m, 1.125rem);
line-height: var(--text-body-m--line-height, 2rem);
color: var(--color-body-primary, #444444);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-061a57a9e9c1fb635b74e6c42dccd5af" class="builder-061a57a9e9c1fb635b74e6c42dccd5af builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-38b5572853c3af5f45602359be1737bb" class="builder-38b5572853c3af5f45602359be1737bb builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 1</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-c09839cffdfabb6237f2ff93aa0f099e" class="builder-c09839cffdfabb6237f2ff93aa0f099e builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 4/6/2021</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-c0f03c721cffcb5d8a6fc8cdb764552b" class="builder-c0f03c721cffcb5d8a6fc8cdb764552b builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Orion Platform versions 2019.4.2 and 2020.2.4 were designed to<strong> protect you from both SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA—and have also been digitally re-signed with our newly obtained digital code-signing certificates. </strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-d09c6893c094b9bdad3afdf08a36af5a" class="builder-d09c6893c094b9bdad3afdf08a36af5a builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Orion Platform version 2020.2.5 adds to these enhancements with additional security fixes and protections. We recommend you upgrade to the latest available release (2020.2.5) as soon as is practical. For more information, review the Release Notes <a href="https://documentation.solarwinds.com/en/Success_Center/orionplatform/content/release_notes/orion_platform_2020-2-5_release_notes.htm" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>, and KB article <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Remote-Code-Execution-CVE-Frequently-Asked-Questions?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-701404e1d88e8d667fde447ba2f38b02" class="builder-701404e1d88e8d667fde447ba2f38b02 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you have recently upgraded to 2020.2.1 HF2 or 2019.4 HF 6, you are <strong>also protected from SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA</strong>. However, as part of our response to the <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/sa-overview/securityadvisory" dld="SUNBURST vulnerability embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">SUNBURST vulnerability</a>, the code-signing certificate used by SolarWinds to sign the affected software versions was revoked <strong>March 8, 2021</strong>, and you may experience performance issues if you do not apply the more recent updates. Please visit our <strong>SolarWinds New Digital Code-Signing Certificate </strong>page at<strong> </strong><a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/sa-overview/new-digital-certificate" dld="solarwinds.com/trust-center/new-digital-certificate embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">solarwinds.com/trust-center/new-digital-certificate</a> for more information.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-8557cef3dc9a34040ebff094995df0eb {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-8557cef3dc9a34040ebff094995df0eb" class="builder-8557cef3dc9a34040ebff094995df0eb builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;

    } </style><div builder-id="builder-61249ef52c35a46e86430f3fc123fd3b" class="builder-61249ef52c35a46e86430f3fc123fd3b builder-block"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><h3>Am I safe if I disconnect my Orion server from the internet?</h3></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-4b4a16f9a1b910d1bf8534855f66575f {
    font-size: var(--text-body-m, 1.125rem);
line-height: var(--text-body-m--line-height, 2rem);
color: var(--color-body-primary, #444444);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-4b4a16f9a1b910d1bf8534855f66575f" class="builder-4b4a16f9a1b910d1bf8534855f66575f builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-813f2673c2afed3c8541c3d72a60b7e4" class="builder-813f2673c2afed3c8541c3d72a60b7e4 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 2</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-9eb96738d753db700b1370cc1af0f65b" class="builder-9eb96738d753db700b1370cc1af0f65b builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 4/6/2021</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-ac4fb3fa229a2e712b4f678c2ab5a2f5" class="builder-ac4fb3fa229a2e712b4f678c2ab5a2f5 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>Am I safe if I disconnect my Orion server from the internet?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-2b6fd551840a09be73abd56baf4b58c0" class="builder-2b6fd551840a09be73abd56baf4b58c0 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Disconnecting any product from the internet can reduce your attack surface. The Orion Platform is fully functional without an internet connection. Based on our investigations to date, SUNBURST requires an internet connection to be activated and disconnecting your Orion server from the internet should immediately protect your environment from SUNBURST. The vulnerability in the product that allows for the deployment of SUPERNOVA, however, may still be exploited by a malicious actor who accesses your network from inside and not over the internet. Disconnecting from the internet will limit the ability of an external actor exploiting this vulnerability over an internet connection.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-3ea384e46e02983c818082ed03e1c101" class="builder-3ea384e46e02983c818082ed03e1c101 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>IMPORTANT NOTE:</strong> Disconnecting from the internet is not a recommended substitute for upgrading your Orion software to versions 2019.4.2, 2020.2.4, or 2020.2.5, or applying appropriate patches to older versions if a full upgrade is not possible at this time.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-5dacfd6c0566a77e88800387638424c5 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-5dacfd6c0566a77e88800387638424c5" class="builder-5dacfd6c0566a77e88800387638424c5 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
}</style><div class="builder-blocks min-w-0 props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284" builder-path="component.options.navItems.2.blocks" builder-parent-id="builder-1a7c1c84170760d0fb8acd80ed0e491b"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div id="how-is-solarwinds-addressing-sunburst-and-supernova" builder-id="builder-799fb5589c2d28fe817e9ace9bf39a81" class="builder-799fb5589c2d28fe817e9ace9bf39a81 builder-block"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-1df6eff5b95afdaa0e16719b43f35e13 {

    font-size: var(--text-body-m, 1.125rem);
line-height: var(--text-body-m--line-height, 2rem);
color: var(--color-body-primary, #444444);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-b37a2ade0f2eb90e3f27559e228ef85c" class="builder-b37a2ade0f2eb90e3f27559e228ef85c builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-eded8920f2a4e118108eda484c92accf" class="builder-eded8920f2a4e118108eda484c92accf builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 3</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-bbc6285d8c3dc0de9c9f1d975f8f9e44" class="builder-bbc6285d8c3dc0de9c9f1d975f8f9e44 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 4/6/2021</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-55d7e6381dab89ba359c282590e68082" class="builder-55d7e6381dab89ba359c282590e68082 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>How has SolarWinds addressed SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-53c9a0829decf7d16b0f10a1ac471247" class="builder-53c9a0829decf7d16b0f10a1ac471247 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>We have:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-2a22d5a73322c6e7889a4ec162448b4f" class="builder-2a22d5a73322c6e7889a4ec162448b4f builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>removed the software builds known to be affected by SUNBURST from our download sites;</li><li>provided software updates, which include security enhancements, that are designed to address both SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA in supported versions of the Orion server; and</li><li>provided a fix for customers on unsupported versions further described below.</li></ul></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-5ab7c21621bd99ee89f7ecb3a6caf8b6" class="builder-5ab7c21621bd99ee89f7ecb3a6caf8b6 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>RECOMMENDED ACTIONS</strong><br/></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-7eb835f329db6c8bbc642c5553c7a689" class="builder-7eb835f329db6c8bbc642c5553c7a689 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>As part of our response to the <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/sa-overview/securityadvisory" dld="SUNBURST vulnerability embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">SUNBURST vulnerability</a>, the code-signing certificate used by SolarWinds to sign the affected software versions was revoked <strong>March 8, 2021</strong>. Please visit our <strong>SolarWinds New Digital Code-Signing Certificate </strong>page at<strong> </strong><a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/sa-overview/new-digital-certificate" dld="solarwinds.com/trust-center/new-digital-certificate embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">solarwinds.com/trust-center/new-digital-certificate</a> for more information.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-5c87e3450b2a6b73f3744f98b0e08a2c" class="builder-5c87e3450b2a6b73f3744f98b0e08a2c builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-b65f9f3db1450d2f7a81918ffa5ef305" class="builder-b65f9f3db1450d2f7a81918ffa5ef305 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>We <u>strongly</u> encourage customers to upgrade their Orion products to the latest versions available in the Customer Portal at <a href="https://customerportal.solarwinds.com/" dld="customerportal.solarwinds.com embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">customerportal.solarwinds.com</a> that are designed to protect you from SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA. Doing so allows you to the get the full benefit of our updates, improvements, and enhancements.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-a5b9f3239feb538065e6ed41900eafe0" class="builder-a5b9f3239feb538065e6ed41900eafe0 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>If you <strong>have</strong> already upgraded to <strong>2020.2.5</strong>, additional security fixes and protections unrelated to SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA are included in this version. For more information, review the Release Notes <a href="https://documentation.solarwinds.com/en/Success_Center/orionplatform/content/release_notes/orion_platform_2020-2-5_release_notes.htm" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>, and KB article <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Remote-Code-Execution-CVE-Frequently-Asked-Questions?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</li><li>If you have upgraded to <strong>2020.2.4</strong> or <strong>2019.4 HF 2</strong>, both the SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA vulnerabilities have been addressed in these versions—and they have also been digitally re-signed by newly obtained digital code-signing certificates.</li><li>If you <strong>have not</strong> already upgraded to <strong>2020.2.5</strong>,<strong> 2020.2.4</strong>, or <strong>2019.4.2</strong>, follow the guidance identified for your current version of your Orion Platform products below to help ensure the security of your environment.</li></ul></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-baba8a6c34f9920715ac27549352a0a3" class="builder-baba8a6c34f9920715ac27549352a0a3 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Here are the steps to take:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-ee9e3ca0253e4fa4418dcb14c66c810a" class="builder-ee9e3ca0253e4fa4418dcb14c66c810a builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>	1. Identify the version of the Orion Platform products you are using by reviewing the directions on how to check that <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Determine-which-version-of-a-SolarWinds-Orion-product-I-have-installed?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a> or refer to the image below. To check which updates you have applied, please go <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Verify-hotfixes-that-have-been-installed?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-962c176e6a28237dd0610d6707dbe3a8" class="builder-962c176e6a28237dd0610d6707dbe3a8 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>	From Orion Web Console </strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-4ddcd818f47c20e0a50bdff2c0f78387" class="builder-4ddcd818f47c20e0a50bdff2c0f78387 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>	All product versions are displayed in the footer of the Orion Web Console login page. See the example below of <strong>2019.4 HF 4</strong>:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-2333e81c3a3b84f1ec245ea2e1c37b9e" class="builder-2333e81c3a3b84f1ec245ea2e1c37b9e builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><figure style="margin: 0"><img src="https://p1.aprimocdn.net/solarwinds/08b66e51-2473-4284-8926-b3990119d644/sw-footer.png_Original%20file.png" height="65" alt="sw-footer.png" class="embedded-asset" content-type-uid="sys_assets" type="asset" asset-alt="SolarWinds footer Orion Web Console login page" width="657" max-height="65" style="max-height: 65px; height: 65px; width: auto" data-sys-asset-filelink="https://images.contentstack.io/v3/assets/blt28ff6c4a2cf43126/blt985cf5300bd75143/66a017929cd6fdee9ef234cf/sw-footer.png" data-sys-asset-uid="blt985cf5300bd75143" data-sys-asset-filename="sw-footer.png" data-sys-asset-contenttype="image/png" data-sys-asset-alt="SolarWinds footer Orion Web Console login page" data-sys-asset-position="none" sys-style-type="display"/></figure></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-de00530bbc5a6d49de5b5a866350c720" class="builder-de00530bbc5a6d49de5b5a866350c720 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-a133492b1583c6ddc2cbf587462fc1dc" class="builder-a133492b1583c6ddc2cbf587462fc1dc builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>	2. We recommend taking the steps related to your use of your version of the SolarWinds Orion Platform per the table below:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-cba1b86d52ee0a3bcb6d4f6c996a1b3d" class="builder-cba1b86d52ee0a3bcb6d4f6c996a1b3d builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you’re unable to upgrade at this time, we have provided a script that customers can install to temporarily protect their environment against SUPERNOVA***. The script is available at <a href="https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip" dld="https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip</a>. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply this script.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-23b79676ec9b26061e48ccb14e759222" class="builder-23b79676ec9b26061e48ccb14e759222 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-65ee8d5c06a972cbb515f2fb778f036f" class="builder-65ee8d5c06a972cbb515f2fb778f036f builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-629bbce0e4a4690b448e414113bf6034 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);

  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-629bbce0e4a4690b448e414113bf6034" class="builder-629bbce0e4a4690b448e414113bf6034 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {

script<strong>*** </strong>go to <a href="https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip" dld="https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip While this version is not impacted by the SUNBURST vulnerability, it is the first version in which we have seen activity from the attacker at this time. Subsequent releases 2019.4 HF 1, 2019.4 HF 2, 2019.4 HF 3, and 2019.4 HF 4 did not include either test modifications contained in the 2019.4 version or the SUNBURST vulnerability contained in 2019.4 HF 5, 2020.2 unpatched, and 2020.2 HF 1.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-1217815269e8fc18a3fd8dc882e4ad8b" class="builder-1217815269e8fc18a3fd8dc882e4ad8b builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>**<strong> </strong>If you apply a SUPERNOVA security patch per the above chart, please visit <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Use-a-script-to-verify-that-the-Supernova-patch-was-applied-to-all-Orion-web-servers?language=en_US" dld="this KB article embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">this KB article</a> to validate the patch was applied to all Orion Platform web servers. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply the respective patch.<br/></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-65329f69b119d47b449dd3188cf38803" class="builder-65329f69b119d47b449dd3188cf38803 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>*** </strong>If you used the SUPERNOVA Mitigation Script to address the Supernova vulnerability, use the guidance in the document within that package to confirm the temporary patch. Please note that this script has only been tested down to NPM 11.x. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply this script.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-cd344c60dddd6326de2ddcc4cb60e547" class="builder-cd344c60dddd6326de2ddcc4cb60e547 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>All recommended upgrade</strong> versions are currently available at <a href="https://customerportal.solarwinds.com/" dld="customerportal.solarwinds.com embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">customerportal.solarwinds.com</a>. All hotfix updates are cumulative and can be installed from any earlier version. There is no need to install previously released updates.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-5350e2c1cd67bee0438944796a2d28a2" class="builder-5350e2c1cd67bee0438944796a2d28a2 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>Based on our investigations to date, we are not aware that the SUNBURST vulnerability affects other versions of our Orion Platform products. Also, while we are still investigating our non-Orion Platform products, we have not seen any evidence that they are impacted by the SUNBURST vulnerability or the SUPERNOVA malware.</strong></p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-ed8174ecaa46c48e4a6b80a4c885a1c3 {

data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-efacf52e19c70fbbde7e5d37ac568973" class="builder-efacf52e19c70fbbde7e5d37ac568973 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>What are SUNSPOT, TEARDROP, and RAINDROP?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-16461c4c03f783d57ba70a00bcbd15a0" class="builder-16461c4c03f783d57ba70a00bcbd15a0 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>SUNSPOT, TEARDROP, and RAINDROP <strong>are NOT new vulnerabilities</strong> within our products as some reports in the media have indicated, but instead, they are <strong>elements of the SUNBURST attack chain.</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-63c611261e2f4340a9018079c8c84719" class="builder-63c611261e2f4340a9018079c8c84719 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><br/>SUNSPOT, for example, is the means by which the attackers injected the SUNBURST backdoor during the build process of the Orion Platform, while TEARDROP and RAINDROP are reportedly malware loaders that could be deployed using the SUNBURST backdoor.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-739277883b19934e59d30078e8af157e {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;

  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;

</style><div builder-id="builder-4fdb3d6093d343f093fb6712765cee7a" class="builder-4fdb3d6093d343f093fb6712765cee7a builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 6</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-0ad3f87749bff2bf784601ebda7f34d6" data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-6bde26112112fb409220ec6d2a39e947" class="builder-6bde26112112fb409220ec6d2a39e947 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>How is SolarWinds responding to these security vulnerabilities?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-e3da537fb614bbfd49cb4e9badb1c233" class="builder-e3da537fb614bbfd49cb4e9badb1c233 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>As announced by SolarWinds President and CEO Sudhakar Ramakrishna in his Orange Matter blog, <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/blog/our-plan-for-a-safer-solarwinds-and-customer-community" dld="Our Plan for a Safer SolarWinds and Customer Community embed" dlt="Glossary Rail"><em>Our Plan for a Safer SolarWinds and Customer Community</em></a>, we are taking key steps to ensure the security and integrity of the software that we deliver to customers.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-1d8006b5ca50028b62a17f290e96819c" class="builder-1d8006b5ca50028b62a17f290e96819c builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>All new hotfixes and patches we have released since SUNBURST was detected have undergone these new levels of scrutiny. SolarWinds is increasing existing actions and taking additional actions across the enterprise to further harden and improve the security of our environment and products:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-285fd9cf2b27c1a6497b8d9417190742" class="builder-285fd9cf2b27c1a6497b8d9417190742 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>Increased hardening of build environment and build pipeline</li><li>Auditing and surveillance</li><li>Environmental reviews by third-party cybersecurity experts</li><li>Release code penetration testing (pentesting)</li><li>Increased scanning of downloadable bits</li><li>Scanning and analysis of source code and build output</li><li>Refreshing of all employee and contractor credentials and credential security and elevating access restriction</li><li>Re-signing of all release code with new certificates</li></ul></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-9c08346de91b84f8669b76d57b04b82b" class="builder-9c08346de91b84f8669b76d57b04b82b builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>In addition, we’re elevating our ongoing architectural focus on continually improving the way we design, build, and support our products.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-d24273e3b9839230a0e8108d124c67b4 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-d24273e3b9839230a0e8108d124c67b4" class="builder-d24273e3b9839230a0e8108d124c67b4 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {

  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-a21a636133411899d79eb8860a241751" class="builder-a21a636133411899d79eb8860a241751 builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-b3e155db9957f5afac52b8f443c16001" class="builder-b3e155db9957f5afac52b8f443c16001 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 7</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-9884948e28f05ff76aca80cbd03da30c" class="builder-9884948e28f05ff76aca80cbd03da30c builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 4/6/2021</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-a80614cbbd31f7d5eb5ebe134edea28e" class="builder-a80614cbbd31f7d5eb5ebe134edea28e builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>What actions should I take?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-7d8b7ba87221d90c4e872489e6a4e4e3" class="builder-7d8b7ba87221d90c4e872489e6a4e4e3 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>As part of our response to the <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/sa-overview/securityadvisory" dld="SUNBURST vulnerability embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">SUNBURST vulnerability</a>, the code-signing certificate used by SolarWinds to sign the affected software versions was revoked <strong>March 8, 2021</strong>. Please visit our <strong>SolarWinds New Digital Code-Signing Certificate </strong>page at<strong> </strong><a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/sa-overview/new-digital-certificate" dld="solarwinds.com/trust-center/new-digital-certificate embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">solarwinds.com/trust-center/new-digital-certificate</a> for more information.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-ba61fb89f86c2b61b6638bd9642b8542" class="builder-ba61fb89f86c2b61b6638bd9642b8542 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>We encourage all customers to run only supported versions of our products and to upgrade to the latest versions to the get the full benefit of our updates, improvements and enhancements. The latest versions of our products are available in the Customer Portal at <a href="https://customerportal.solarwinds.com/" dld="customerportal.solarwinds.com embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">customerportal.solarwinds.com</a>. Based on our investigation, we recommend that all customers of Orion Platform products apply the latest updates applicable to the version of the product they have deployed.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-ad70af74820fc734346831e49722a06a" class="builder-ad70af74820fc734346831e49722a06a builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>To take advantage of our latest available security updates protections for the products you have deployed, we recommend all active maintenance customers of Orion Platform products <strong>upgrade to version 2020.2.5</strong> as soon as possible. For more information, review the Release Notes <a href="https://documentation.solarwinds.com/en/Success_Center/orionplatform/content/release_notes/orion_platform_2020-2-5_release_notes.htm" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>, and KB article <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Remote-Code-Execution-CVE-Frequently-Asked-Questions?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-5b9f4a00f053873ec4bddb8ba02fb686" class="builder-5b9f4a00f053873ec4bddb8ba02fb686 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Orion Platform versions 2019.4.2 and 2020.2.4 were designed to protect you from both the SUNBURST vulnerability and the SUPERNOVA malware and have been re-signed with our newly obtained digital-signing certificate.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-2ff8ad25163380ec8b18256fd3aed220" class="builder-2ff8ad25163380ec8b18256fd3aed220 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>You may need to synchronize your license prior to applying the hotfix. </strong>Please follow the steps <a href="https://documentation.solarwinds.com/en/success_center/orionplatform/Content/Core-Centralized-Licensing-Synchronize.htm" dld="here ebed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a> to kick off the synchronization of your license. If you have disabled outward communication from your Orion license, please follow the “Activate License Offline” section from <a href="https://documentation.solarwinds.com/en/Success_Center/orionplatform/Content/Core-Centralized-Licensing-Activate-Licenses.htm" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>. Once you have successfully synched your license, please run the installer to install the hotfix.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-c04eb652cab5d8bccf37cb79f5c0d8bf" class="builder-c04eb652cab5d8bccf37cb79f5c0d8bf builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you’re unable to upgrade at this time, we have provided a script that customers can install to temporarily protect their environment against SUPERNOVA. The script is available at <a href="https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip" dld="https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip</a>. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply this script.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-df593ace18e3412985b0649c755f1a40" class="builder-df593ace18e3412985b0649c755f1a40 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>We also recommend customers review our <strong>Secure Configuration for the Orion Platform</strong> guide <a href="https://documentation.solarwinds.com/en/Success_Center/orionplatform/content/core-secure-configuration.htm" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a> for additional best practices.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-0273084452cac11e7a588be7e298136c" class="builder-0273084452cac11e7a588be7e298136c builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-16aae7b3e7ec5b620d27ff075d273627 {

Do these alerts mean that I am still at risk?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-285f8ccb69c13277c0da20d23425d0a7" class="builder-285f8ccb69c13277c0da20d23425d0a7 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Not necessarily. We do not have evidence to suggest that the mere presence of these old Orion installers puts you at risk.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-4b7aa624c28bcb4e283607f887f96b06" class="builder-4b7aa624c28bcb4e283607f887f96b06 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you’ve already upgraded to 2019.4.2, 2020.2.4, or 2020.2.5, the affected SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll was removed and replaced from the runtime area, prohibiting it from executing further. However, depending on the path taken to upgrade, some old versions of the .dll or MSIs were not fully removed.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-8bc93766abc348bf78de04864d75103a" class="builder-8bc93766abc348bf78de04864d75103a builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>To eliminate these alerts, we recommend that you remove those old installer files by following the steps outlined <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Anti-Virus-Still-Detecting-Affected-SolarWinds-Orion-Core-BusinessLayer-dll-after-Upgrade?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-8f4e329593ce3b8ca6345cb11562e286 {

CERT issued <a href="https://us-cert.cisa.gov/ncas/alerts/aa20-352a" dld="Alert (AA20-352A) embed" dlt="Glossary Rail" target="_blank">Alert (AA20-352A</a>), titled <em>Advanced Persistent Threat Compromise of Government Agencies, Critical Infrastructure, and Private Sector Organizations</em>, as an update to ED 21-01 on December 17, 2020, based on our coordination with the agency. DHS released Supplemental Guidance to ED 21-01 on December 18, 2020, CERT revised its Alert AA20-352A on December 19, 2020, DHS released Supplemental Guidance v2 on December 30, 2020, and released Supplemental Guidance v3 on January 6, 2021 as part of our ongoing coordination with the agency. Additionally, CISA released a malware analysis report of SUPERNOVA on January 27, 2021.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-f0cc2ea0e496f36288538677bde8859e" class="builder-f0cc2ea0e496f36288538677bde8859e builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>The latest information can be found on the<strong> CISA Supply Chain Compromise</strong> page at <a href="https://www.cisa.gov/supply-chain-compromise" dld="https://www.cisa.gov/supply-chain-compromise embed" dlt="Glossary Rail" target="_blank">https://www.cisa.gov/supply-chain-compromise</a>.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-a430215ceac9d05ac0b597982a9fbca0" class="builder-a430215ceac9d05ac0b597982a9fbca0 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>The latest release, Orion Platform version <strong>2020.2.5</strong> is now available in the SolarWinds Customer Portal at <a href="https://customerportal.solarwinds.com/" dld="customerportal.solarwinds.com embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">customerportal.solarwinds.com</a>, in accordance with ED 21-01.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-2a9d06fa10e74facc5c6610e4f6c5a93 {

    font-size: var(--text-body-m, 1.125rem);
line-height: var(--text-body-m--line-height, 2rem);
color: var(--color-body-primary, #444444);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-638e87ceb5d50ef1b9eff2f8060bed56" class="builder-638e87ceb5d50ef1b9eff2f8060bed56 builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-56e1b5af2680e10aaaf252bf9e9aa800" class="builder-56e1b5af2680e10aaaf252bf9e9aa800 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 14</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-f26463d5257906a4f559870601cecf91" class="builder-f26463d5257906a4f559870601cecf91 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 2/2/2021</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-4d30ae9393a236fcca6dcd52c143b3ee" class="builder-4d30ae9393a236fcca6dcd52c143b3ee builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>Why does CISA recommend users split out the web server from the Orion Application server?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-3021c8f0c6deb63f664b58669f6734c8" class="builder-3021c8f0c6deb63f664b58669f6734c8 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>By splitting out the web server from the application server, you can leverage different controls to further secure your deployment. Orion Platform users need access to the web server (port 443), which could have a large surface area, and the web server does <em>not</em> need to have network access to the monitored devices – only the pollers (including the main poller) need network access. By deploying a separate web server and shutting down and/or blocking access to the web console on the main poller, you can put a firebreak between the web interface used by users and the monitored systems.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-c977c827c70dd9611d8743d10968a9ad {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-c977c827c70dd9611d8743d10968a9ad" class="builder-c977c827c70dd9611d8743d10968a9ad builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
}</style><div class="builder-blocks min-w-0 props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284" builder-path="component.options.navItems.14.blocks" builder-parent-id="builder-1a7c1c84170760d0fb8acd80ed0e491b"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div id="what-is-solarwinds-doing-to-help-find-a-solution" builder-id="builder-4d6cc294cc86219de3cf1a7615c9df14" class="builder-4d6cc294cc86219de3cf1a7615c9df14 builder-block"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-56de50b97ca055de377fb852f901b321 {

As we shared in our <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/blog/our-plan-for-a-safer-solarwinds-and-customer-community" dld="recent update embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">recent update</a>, we're partnering with multiple industry-leading cybersecurity experts to strengthen our systems, further enhance our product development processes, and adapt the ways that we deliver powerful, affordable, and secure solutions to our customers. Read more on the latest findings from our investigation of SUNBURST <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/blog/new-findings-from-our-investigation-of-sunburst" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-27169fbfa2995b4070d376217689cf16" class="builder-27169fbfa2995b4070d376217689cf16 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>We have retained third-party cybersecurity experts to assist in an investigation of these matters, including whether a vulnerability in the Orion Platform products was exploited as a point of any infiltration of customer systems, and in the development of appropriate mitigation and remediation plans. SolarWinds is cooperating and sharing information with partners, vendors, law enforcement, and the intelligence and government agencies around the world to assist in investigations related to this incident.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-b2f9cd1d37054124c4fb817375844e3b" class="builder-b2f9cd1d37054124c4fb817375844e3b builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>We are solely focused on our customers and the industry we serve.</strong> Our top priority has been to take all steps necessary to ensure that our and our customers’ environments are secure. We are taking extraordinary measures to accomplish this goal. We shared all of our proprietary code libraries that we believed to have been affected by SUNBURST to give security professionals the information they needed to do their research. We also have had numerous conversations with security professionals to further assist them in their research. We were very pleased and proud to hear that colleagues in the industry discovered a “killswitch” that will prevent the malicious code from being used to create a compromise.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-d5b78955f3e620a0dc9b3fa0b43b656f {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-d5b78955f3e620a0dc9b3fa0b43b656f" class="builder-d5b78955f3e620a0dc9b3fa0b43b656f builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
}</style><div class="builder-blocks min-w-0 props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284" builder-path="component.options.navItems.15.blocks" builder-parent-id="builder-1a7c1c84170760d0fb8acd80ed0e491b"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div id="what-if-i-can-t-upgrade-right-now-how-do-i-ensure-the-security-of-my-orion-server" builder-id="builder-92759fc479f756f09d046bb003b1be06" class="builder-92759fc479f756f09d046bb003b1be06 builder-block"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-d6a2f5b7be6aedfe4bfa9852a073a358 {

  }
    } </style><div builder-id="builder-d6a2f5b7be6aedfe4bfa9852a073a358" class="builder-d6a2f5b7be6aedfe4bfa9852a073a358 builder-block"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><h3>What if I can’t upgrade right now? How do I ensure the security of my Orion server?</h3></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-6e098c6720240814ceccc31cf4c560bc {
    font-size: var(--text-body-m, 1.125rem);
line-height: var(--text-body-m--line-height, 2rem);
color: var(--color-body-primary, #444444);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-6e098c6720240814ceccc31cf4c560bc" class="builder-6e098c6720240814ceccc31cf4c560bc builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-1fd98ea8f750f84b4103acfe2fd6f197" class="builder-1fd98ea8f750f84b4103acfe2fd6f197 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 16</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-44522fe409cefa02db237f26ad226643" class="builder-44522fe409cefa02db237f26ad226643 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 12/26/2020</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-863a2042c184aa4438ede10964fa4561" class="builder-863a2042c184aa4438ede10964fa4561 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>What if I can’t upgrade right now? How do I ensure the security of my Orion server?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-ed030c925d83b39b42522c650135ceb9" class="builder-ed030c925d83b39b42522c650135ceb9 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you can’t upgrade immediately, please follow the guidelines available <a href="https://documentation.solarwinds.com/en/Success_Center/orionplatform/content/core-secure-configuration.htm" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a> for improving the security of your Orion Platform instance. Primary mitigation steps include:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-909229672a55091ed92597ef4727e505" class="builder-909229672a55091ed92597ef4727e505 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>Ensure the Orion Platform is installed behind a firewall,</li><li>Disable internet access for the Orion Platform, and</li><li>Limit the ports and connections to only what is required to operate your platform.</li></ul></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-0e2c74dc055b8fb299dc8c175441714b" class="builder-0e2c74dc055b8fb299dc8c175441714b builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you’re unable to upgrade at this time, we have provided a script that customers can install to temporarily protect their environment against SUPERNOVA. The script is available at <a href="https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip" dld="https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">https://downloads.solarwinds.com/solarwinds/Support/SupernovaMitigation.zip</a>. Please note that this script has only been tested down to NPM 11.x. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply this script.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-fe37ba211e7a4e9a8216528eee61fe19 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-fe37ba211e7a4e9a8216528eee61fe19" class="builder-fe37ba211e7a4e9a8216528eee61fe19 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {

  align-items: stretch;

How can I do that?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-bf9184695c43fc5056737e39c16b7557" class="builder-bf9184695c43fc5056737e39c16b7557 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>A list of SolarWinds Checksums is available <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/product-checksum" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a> for your reference. If you’re unaware of how to perform this validation, one such method is using “Get-FileHash” from the Microsoft PowerShell Utility documented <a href="https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.utility/get-filehash?view=powershell-7.1" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail" target="_blank">here</a>.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-0f8d80ad6f762ae9f35fd42392b5e323" class="builder-0f8d80ad6f762ae9f35fd42392b5e323 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>As an example, the Solarwinds-Orion-NPM-2020.2.1-OfflineInstaller.iso was obtained through the Customer Portal:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-d8d3c30e7b8273327d934b4cd713e017" class="builder-d8d3c30e7b8273327d934b4cd713e017 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><figure style="margin: 0"><img src="https://p1.aprimocdn.net/solarwinds/9e3048da-9b20-4f15-8eac-b399010cded4/release-downloads.png_Original%20file.png" height="170" alt="release-downloads.png" class="embedded-asset" content-type-uid="sys_assets" type="asset" asset-alt="Release Downloads" width="673" max-height="170" style="max-height: 170px; Here is an example output and matching checksum available <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/product-checksum" dld="below embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">below</a>. Note: A response may take a few moments.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-46ef33fe55d4062c73aecf7e1e4584d1"   }    </style><div builder-id="builder-e125ec9fdd5751006f86ceec3b7953bd" class="builder-e125ec9fdd5751006f86ceec3b7953bd builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-8560a484ac2d9417f6c4efee81324565" class="builder-8560a484ac2d9417f6c4efee81324565 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>*</strong>As a part of the ongoing investigation, we have determined that Orion Platform version 2019.4 <u>unpatched</u>, released in October 2019, contained test modifications to the code base. While this version is not impacted by the SUNBURST vulnerability, it is the first version in which we have seen activity from the attacker at this time. Subsequent releases 2019.4 HF 1, 2019.4 HF 2, 2019.4 HF 3, and 2019.4 HF 4 did not include either test modifications contained in the 2019.4 version or the SUNBURST vulnerability contained in 2019.4 HF 5, 2020.2 with no hotfix and 2020.2 HF 1.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-c48cb2e5f26e2a814d085cdb37f7ffd6" class="builder-c48cb2e5f26e2a814d085cdb37f7ffd6 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>** If you apply a SUPERNOVA security patch per the above chart, please visit <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Use-a-script-to-verify-that-the-Supernova-patch-was-applied-to-all-Orion-web-servers?language=en_US" dld="this KB article embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">this KB article</a> to validate the patch was applied to all Orion Platform web servers. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply the respective patch.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-dec37d0d2ceeafe996d4aa50a6017bad" class="builder-dec37d0d2ceeafe996d4aa50a6017bad builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>*** </strong>If you use the SUPERNOVA Mitigation Script to address the Supernova vulnerability, use the guidance in the document within that package to confirm the temporary patch. Please note that this script has only been tested down to NPM 11.x. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply this script.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-c908aa519634f7295fc9c19851b7618f {

OR What are you doing to prevent future incidents moving forward?</h3></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-e9fd1d77f65246b4cf7852819cef7e3e {

OR What are you doing to prevent future incidents moving forward?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-e7733f09b6d9b0057c90406b88295160" class="builder-e7733f09b6d9b0057c90406b88295160 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Our investigations are ongoing, but since the vulnerabilities related to SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA were discovered, we have reviewed our environment, giving an initial focus on ensuring the security of our build environment, including our source code repositories. We have reviewed the architecture of the build environment, the privileged and non-privileged users that have access to the build environment, and the network surrounding the build environment.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-8008ccb0881afaaea64b62cf017d3c88" class="builder-8008ccb0881afaaea64b62cf017d3c88 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>We have retained third-party cybersecurity experts to assist in an investigation of these matters, including whether the vulnerabilities were exploited as a point of any infiltration of any customer systems, and in the development of appropriate mitigation and remediation plans to help ensure future releases are protected. We are also cooperating and sharing information with our partners, vendors, and law enforcement, intelligence, and government agencies around the world to assist in investigations and to avoid vulnerabilities in future releases.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-82664f13885e82ca3aec04e8bcce1f2c" class="builder-82664f13885e82ca3aec04e8bcce1f2c builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Our investigations are ongoing, and we will continue to focus on ensuring that our environments are secure and protected. Read more about our investigation <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/blog/new-findings-from-our-investigation-of-sunburst" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>, and read more about the actions we're taking moving forward <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/blog/our-plan-for-a-safer-solarwinds-and-customer-community" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-6ae6946ffdd247fb2fba56b7f150ad4f {

SolarWinds has always been committed to working with our customers and other organizations to identify and remediate any vulnerabilities across our product portfolio in a responsible way. Trustwave is a leading threat detection company that responsibly reports product vulnerabilities to companies as they are discovered prior to broader announcement to allow companies to develop remediations to protect their customers.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-46b9b4b185e8572ff0219d875cb0197a" class="builder-46b9b4b185e8572ff0219d875cb0197a builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>The vulnerabilities announced by Trustwave concerning the Orion® Platform were addressed in Orion Platform version 2020.2.4, released January 25, 2021. The vulnerabilities concerning Serv-U were addressed in Serv-U version 15.2.2 HF1, released January 22, 2021. To review the list of currently supported versions of these products, please review the <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Currently-supported-software-versions?language=en_US" dld="documentation embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">documentation</a>.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-19da9790e996e97a800d0bd7d2d77993" class="builder-19da9790e996e97a800d0bd7d2d77993 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Trustwave identified three vulnerabilities, two in the Orion Platform and one in our Serv-U file transfer product. To our knowledge, none of these vulnerabilities have been exploited. The three vulnerabilities could be described as follows:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-a4e8f94a559582cfeb1871bf803a337a" class="builder-a4e8f94a559582cfeb1871bf803a337a builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>The first, which affects the Orion Platform, allows for an unprivileged user to extract passwords from the SQL Server database.</li><li>The second allows a remote user to execute a command against the Microsoft Message Queue service used by the Orion Platform.</li><li>The third allows an attacker to gain access to the Serv-U FTP server by creating an unauthorized user account.</li></ul></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-32d1c800f7e06facce0cf4c6adc107b2 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-32d1c800f7e06facce0cf4c6adc107b2" class="builder-32d1c800f7e06facce0cf4c6adc107b2 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {

  align-items: stretch;

This situation was the result of a compromise within an individual company's network unrelated to SolarWinds. That breach enabled attackers to add malicious code to the Orion Platform software instance within the customer's network.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-452221a0323dd358446f3b725226ed9f" class="builder-452221a0323dd358446f3b725226ed9f builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>In November of 2020, Sophos investigated an environment infected with a Ragnar Locker attack in which hundreds of computers were compromised.</li><li>One of the computers compromised in the Ragnar Locker attack was a Windows server hosting the Orion product.</li><li>The threat actor had full administrative access to the Windows server and was able to copy and replace a signed .dll shipped with the Orion Platform software with an unsigned, compromised version.</li><li>This compromised code was not shipped with the Orion Platform software.</li><li>Following the Orion Secure Configuration Guide mitigates the risk of this type of attack.</li></ul></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-5cf42379e9196d7ffea16c38d62a1037 {
    display: flex;

margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
